items.title: Prosperous NT mining town prepares for tough times as Rio Tinto exit looms

children.content: In Australia's tropical north, a mining town is preparing for the departure of its biggest employer, and locals are accusing the mining giant of squeezing out small businesses on its way out.

image.altText: Streets of homes and two large green ovals can be seen in an aerial shot of a remote coastal community.

items.title: NT shadow minister admits underage son drove car, considers resignation

children.content: Manuel Brown has admitted his underage son drove his personal car, one week after failing to disclose the matter when asked by the media, and says he is considering resigning from his shadow portfolios.

image.altText: an aboriginal man wearing a suit in parliament

items.title: Chilling image from January 26 that stops gallery-goers in their tracks

children.content: There is a wide range of artworks among the finalists vying for this year's prestigious National Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art Awards, from photography and painting to multimedia and sculpture.

articles.title: 'Deadly' Indigenous remote artists travel to Sydney

children.content: Remote Indigenous artists from around Australia are in Sydney for the annual National Indigenous Art Fair.

image.altText: Woman putting up an artwork in the exhibition.

gallery.altText: A black-and-white photo of a smiling Aboriginal girl on her dad's shoulders as they march through the city.
A close-up of a framed black-and-white photo of a young Aboriginal girl on her dad's shoulders as they march through the city.
People inside an art gallery inspecting the works on display, many of the paintings feature red dirt and orange hues.

items.title: Secret police recordings of croc wrangler Matt Wright released

children.content: Secretly recorded phone calls and conversations crucial to the criminal case against celebrity crocodile wrangler Matt Wright during his Northern Territory Supreme Court trial have been released to the media.

image.altText: Close-up shot of Matt Wright's side profile as he walks into Darwin Local Court wearing a light blue shirt.

items.title: Former St Mary's Hostel site recognised as key Stolen Generations place

children.content: Former residents say the heritage listing of the St Mary's Hostel site in Alice Springs is an important step towards healing, truth-telling and remembering the children taken there under assimilation-era policies.

image.altText: A woman in a light grey jumper wearing sunglasses stands in front of a large tree

items.title: Why Alice Springs has been dubbed the lesbian capital of Australia

children.content: New research places Alice Springs in the top 10 most inclusive Australian cities for LGBTQIA+ people, cementing the outback town's long-held reputation as the lesbian capital of the country.

gallery.altText: Two women sit in chairs, one with their arm around the other, and a white dog on the lap of the woman on the right

thumbnail.altText: Two women sit in chairs, one with their arm around the other, and a white dog on the lap of the woman on the right

items.title: Taylor Swift marries Travis Kelce as fans celebrate America's 'royal wedding'

children.content: An open-oven-door style heatwave in New York could not stop Taylor Swift's famously enthusiastic fans from showing up for what's been dubbed America's royal wedding.

articles.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wed, spokesperson confirms

children.content: A heavy media presence and barricades surrounded the Madison Square Garden stadium where fans waited in sweltering conditions.

image.altText: Taylor Swift and Kansas City Chiefs tight end Travis Kelce

items.title: Australia's woeful Asian languages literacy is failing us

children.content: As Asian language enrolments wither Australia is failing to make lasting ties within our region.

image.altText: Prabowo and Albanese speak at a podium, both suits and "HMAS CANBERRA" hats.

items.title: Billionaires to every day people: Inquiry exposes extent of online hate

children.content: Everyone from billionaires to politicians and pro-Palestinian Jewish activists told the royal commission of the hate they receive in phone calls and emails.

image.altText: composite image of Julie Inman Grant, steven lowy and sarah Schwartz

items.title: When a fit young man disappears, he leaves behind a trail of strange clues

children.content: When the first major relationship of Jack McLennan's life implodes, he does what plenty of young men do: meets a mate for a quiet beer. But by morning, Jack's disappeared, leaving behind a series of mysterious clues and a devastated family.

gallery.altText: kellie stands in front of house holding sign saying "bring jack home"
the water at sunset at Ficks crossing
Jack McLennan and Carlee Beetham pictured happy together in front of a waterfall

items.title: 'Towelling the bloke up in the cells': Shocking police emails revealed

children.content: Emails reveal an extraordinary exchange between AFP officers after an Indigenous man was allegedly beaten and illegally strip-searched at a Canberra watchhouse.

image.altText: A person's face and police tape and bars in a graphic image.

items.title: Today Show ratings in 'freefall' as Channel Nine looks to replace Karl Stefanovic

children.content: The Today Show's ratings are in decline following the Nine Network's dumping of host Karl Stefanovic. Former head of News and Current Affairs at Seven, Nine, and 10, Peter Meakin says the audience will want to punish Nine, but things can turn around, even in breakfast TV.

image.altText: Karl Stefanovic in a suit, no tie smiling and standing in front of the Crown

items.title: Rethinking drinking as gen Z wakes up to joy of early rising

children.content: Pub nights are becoming far less rowdy as young people swap big nights out for morning run clubs.

image.altText: Crowds outside of Romano's Pub on New Years Eve in the 90s

items.title: The Hollywood reason why McLaren is green and white for the British GP

children.content: Formula One fans will do double takes this weekend as McLaren races in green and white instead of its usual "papaya" orange.

image.altText: A picture of a race car.

items.title: Why Avalon Emerson refuses to stay in one lane

children.content: Avalon Emerson has built a global reputation as a DJ, but she soon found standing still was not for her.

gallery.altText: Avalon Emerson lying in a grassy field in black dress and boots, head resting on arms, wildflowers around.

thumbnail.altText: Avalon Emerson lying in a grassy field in black dress and boots, head resting on arms, wildflowers around.

items.title: Meet the Pokémon GO player who has walked more than 30,000km

children.content: One Pokémon GO fan walked 32,000 km in his search to "catch them all". On the way he found health, friendship and a partner.

image.altText: A young man with glasses smiles and holds up his phone showing a pokemon on it

items.title: ABC 5050 quiz: A July 4 special with an alien invasion, George III on TV and Taylor Swift

children.content: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right; the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

image.altText: Taylor Swift and Post Malone in the music video for her single Fortnight.

items.title: Hunter region to gain $12b train manufacturing hub

children.content: NSW Premier Chris Minns tells a conference of the party faithful that the state's next set of trains will be manufactured "where the original fleet was built".

image.altText: Tangara train

items.title: Farmers turn restaurant food scraps into 'very valuable commodities'

children.content: While diners enjoy their seaside meals, Andrew Collins is happily outside chasing the scraps.

image.altText: A close up of two hands holding a scoop of soil rich with active worms

items.title: Endangered pine forest grown with people's cremated ashes opens

children.content: When 10-year-old Tegan Chen died after accidentally slipping into a rushing creek on a walk, it stirred her parents to set up "living memorials" for others.

image.altText: Woman stands surounded by wollemi pine saplings with sandstone cliffs in the background.

items.title: Boy left with broken eye socket after being hit by e-scooter

children.content: Cooper Blackshaw was not operating the e-scooter that injured him outside his school gates.

image.altText: A boy in a hospital bed with blood and a bandage on his eye

items.title: Tiny forests planted in Sydney to restore urban tree canopy

children.content: Using enriched soil and densely planted trees, the pocket forests can fit into small underutilised spaces owned by councils.

gallery.altText: a small forest surrounded by grass as seen from above

items.title: Three men acquitted of murdering journalist Lyra McKee

children.content: The 29-year-old was shot by a member of a dissident Irish Republican Army splinter group while covering a riot in Derry/Londonderry in 2019.

image.altText: A young woman with short brown hair and glasses looks into the camera

items.title: Indonesian soldiers recover body of US pilot killed in Papua

children.content: A video shows an operation by the Indonesian military to recover the body of an American pilot, shot dead by Papuan separatists in the Papua Highlands.

image.altText: Soldiers in uniform with guns put a dead body on a tarpaulin.

items.title: Interpol issues wanted notice for Monaco bombing suspect

children.content: Investigators are hunting for a woman they say "disguised herself as a man" in connection with in a bomb attack that targeted a wealthy businessman in Monaco.

image.altText: Close up of a woman with dark hair, looking serious, wearing white t-shirt with blue stripes.

items.title: Ange Postecoglou appointed head coach at Saudi club Al-Nassr

children.content: The 60-year-old takes over the Riyadh-based side led by Portugal forward Cristiano Ronaldo.

image.altText: A man looks out onto a football field.

items.title: 175 years ago, Victoria struck gold. 230,000 scars remain

children.content: It has been 175 years since gold was first discovered in Victoria, and now a state government mapping project has discovered that there are far more relics of the gold rush left in the landscape than official records show.

items.title: After 18 months of algal bloom, bird flu has authorities on alert

children.content: While the arrival of bird flu on SA shores is a "huge concern", the state's primary industries minister maintains the government is prepared for the possibility of a wider local outbreak.

image.altText: SA government minister Clare Scriven.

items.title: Kasatkina bows out of Wimbledon as Osaka stuns on and off court

children.content: Daria Kasatkina bows out of Wimbledon to end Australia's run in the women's draw.

items.title: Early morning World Cup viewing parties end in disappointment

children.content: Football fans across the country ride the gamut of emotions at public viewing parties as the Socceroos suffer heartbreaking defeat in the FIFA World Cup, having come from behind to force extra time and penalties against Egypt in their knockout clash in Texas.
